questions.question: You are flying near your favorite VOR. You experience 15° of bearing change in 11 minutes 36 seconds. How far are you from the station?

You and two classmates are flying in the school's practice area. Jess's MH is 130° and is drifting 5° right. Bob's MH is 205° and is drifting 8° left. You have a MH of 270° and are drifting 9° left. You all have a TAS of 110 kts and variation is 5E. What are the true winds aloft?

You just traveled 19.2 NM while fl ying counterclockwise on a DME arc. You intercepted the arc at the 030° radial and left it upon reaching the 340° radial. How far from the VOR were you while on the arc?
What was the aircraft's MH when you had traveled 10 NM on the arc? Assume calm wind.
questions.context: Referenced question 3: You just traveled 19.2 NM while fl ying counterclockwise on a DME arc. You intercepted the arc at the 030° radial and left it upon reaching the 340° radial. How far from the VOR were you while on the arc?

questions.question: You are planning a flight from airport ABC to airport XYZ, 177 NM away. True course is 253°. Winds are 290@17 knots with an OAT of 5°C above standard. If your TAS will be 115 knots, how far from XYZ will you be when the time to continue equals the time to return?
You are cruising at an altitude of 12,500 ft. where the OAT is -10°F and the altimeter setting is 30.32. If the CAS is 104 nautical miles per hour, what is the TAS?
what is true altitude?
questions.context: Referenced question 6: You are cruising at an altitude of 12,500 ft. where the OAT is -10°F and the altimeter setting is 30.32. If the CAS is 104 nautical miles per hour, what is the TAS? [[DIVIDER]] Pertaining to the previous problem, if the ca librated altitude is 12,500 ft.

questions.question: An aircraft has a TAS of 430 knots. What temperature rise is the temp probe experiencing? The probe's recovery coefficient is 0.8.
Given: [[NEXT_LINE]] TC: 212° [[NEXT_LINE]] TAS: 185 knots [[NEXT_LINE]] Wind: 040@28 mph [[NEXT_LINE]] Find: Groundspeed
Given: [[NEXT_LINE]] TC: 100° [[NEXT_LINE]] TH: 078° [[NEXT_LINE]] GS: 138 kph [[NEXT_LINE]] TAS: 130 kph [[NEXT_LINE]] Find: Wind velocity
Given: [[NEXT_LINE]] TC: 350° [[NEXT_LINE]] WCA: 5° L [[NEXT_LINE]] Wind: 305@23 kts [[NEXT_LINE]] Find: Groundspeed
Pertaining to the previous question, what is the TAS?

You are 136 NM due east of the EDY VORTAC. You want to fly over Uncle Buck's house which is 83 km from EDY on the 270° radial. If you want to burn off exactly 28 liters of fuel enroute and your aircraft burns 5.6 Imperial gallons per hour, at what indicated airspeed should you fly? Winds are 120@17 kts (m agnetic), OAT is 68°F and pressure altitude is 6700 ft.
In the previous problem, what is your crosswind component?
questions.context: Referenced question 15: You are 136 NM due east of the EDY VORTAC. You want to fly over Uncle Buck's house which is 83 km from EDY on the 270° radial. If you want to burn off exactly 28 liters of fuel enroute and your aircraft burns 5.6 Imperial gallons per hour, at what indicated airspeed should you fly? Winds are 120@17 kts (m agnetic), OAT is 68°F and pressure altitude is 6700 ft.

questions.question: You have an indicated temperature of -30°C. The temp probe's CT is 1.0, pressure altitude is 25,000 ft. and the Mach meter indicates you are at Mach 0.58. What should your airspeed indicator be indicating?
In the previous problem, what is your TAS?
questions.context: Referenced question 17: You have an indicated temperature of -30°C. The temp probe's CT is 1.0, pressure altitude is 25,000 ft. and the Mach meter indicates you are at Mach 0.58. What should your airspeed indicator be indicating?

questions.question: You and your buddy Roy are each taking an airplane for a trip to get some lunch at an airport 230 NM away. What a perfect opportunity to test the range of your new pocket-size emergency transceiver. You have a TAS of 108 knots and Roy's TAS is 161 knots. The useable range for your transceiver is listed at 35 NM. Assuming you both leave the airport at the same time, how far will you fly before you should exceed the transceiver's range? (Assume calm wind)
How far was Roy from the destination when you actually lost contact with him?
questions.context: Referenced question 19: You and your buddy Roy are each taking an airplane for a trip to get some lunch at an airport 230 NM away. What a perfect opportunity to test the range of your new pocket-size emergency transceiver. You have a TAS of 108 knots and Roy's TAS is 161 knots. The useable range for your transceiver is listed at 35 NM. Assuming you both leave the airport at the same time, how far will you fly before you should exceed the transceiver's range? (Assume calm wind) [[DIVIDER]] (Refer to previous problem) It turns out that you and Roy were actually 40 NM apart before contact was lost.

questions.question: OAT is -35°C and TAS is 790 kts. How many times the speed of sound are you traveling?

You wish to test the DME of the newly commissioned JES VORTAC. You over fly JES and continue outbound on the 100° radial. On you way out you do some sightseeing. After flying 10 NM past JES you fly heading 010° for 15 NM. Then you fly heading 044° for 12 NM. Then you fly heading 314° for 40 NM. What DME should you be indicating at this point? (Assume calm wind.)

You just traveled 48 SM in 11 minutes 18 seconds. OAT is -5°C. Your cruise altitude is 6500 ft. MSL and you are maintaining a magnetic heading of 215°. Winds are 170@28 kts. Variation is 8W. If the altimeter setting is 30.75, what is your true course?
In the previous problem, what would your Mach meter indicate?
questions.context: Referenced question 28: You just traveled 48 SM in 11 minutes 18 seconds. OAT is -5°C. Your cruise altitude is 6500 ft. MSL and you are maintaining a magnetic heading of 215°. Winds are 170@28 kts. Variation is 8W. If the altimeter setting is 30.75, what is your true course?

questions.question: Pertaining to problem #28, what is your calibrated airspeed?

questions.question: An airplane has a current weight of 2750 lbs with a CG of 20% MAC. How much weight must be shifted from aft baggage to forward baggage to bring the CG exactly to the forward CG limit? Forward baggage - station 28 Aft baggage - station 154 MAC - station 58 to station 88 Forward CG limit - 12% MAC
After flying for 37 minutes on a compass heading of 115° you find yourself 13 km off course to the right. If your groundspeed is 138 knots, what CH must you fly to parallel your intended course?

Aircraft Alpha is trying to catch up to and pass aircraft Bravo. Alpha is flying at Mach 1.63 while Bravo is flying at Mach 0.78. OAT is -38°C. When the chase begins the airplanes are 65 NM apart. How long would it take for Alpha to be 37 SM ahead of Bravo?

You are planning your climb profile. From the airport (elevation 1300 ft.) you fly out on a TC of 040°. You fly 105 KTAS & 1800 fpm to 11,000 ft. Then you fly 125 KTAS & 1100 fpm to FL180. Upon reaching FL180 you turn to a TC of 120° and continue to climb at 140 KTAS & 600 fpm to FL270. Average winds are 060@18 knots. How long will it take to reach FL270?
If average fuel burn was 1.6 liters per minute (lpm), how much fuel should be on board upon reaching FL270?
questions.context: Referenced question 34: You are planning your climb profile. From the airport (elevation 1300 ft.) you fly out on a TC of 040°. You fly 105 KTAS & 1800 fpm to 11,000 ft. Then you fly 125 KTAS & 1100 fpm to FL180. Upon reaching FL180 you turn to a TC of 120° and continue to climb at 140 KTAS & 600 fpm to FL270. Average winds are 060@18 knots. How long will it take to reach FL270? [[DIVIDER]] (Refer to previous problem) You departed with 73 gal of fuel on board.

questions.question: You are maintaining a descent gradient of 230 fpnm. True course is 238°. As you pass through 7500' MSL the following conditions exist: Winds are 209@19 kts, CAS is 131 knots, VSI is holding 510 fpm & OAT is 10°C. At the moment you passed through 7500' MSL what was your pressure altitude?
Up at your cruising altitude of 7500 ft. the OAT is -20°C. If the altimeter setting is 1038.6 hPa, what is the density altitude?
An aircraft burns 10.4 gph. If it takes 17 seconds to travel.9 NM, then how much fuel would be consumed while flying 146 NM?
It takes 2 hours 38 minutes to fly 380 SM. What is your speed in mph?
Convert -15°C to Fahrenheit.
How many pounds does 41.3 Imp. Gal of oil weigh?
What is the square root of 178?
You are descending at 4300 fpm. What is this in knots?
How much does 240 lbs of Avgas weigh in kilograms?
Each box contains a dozen quarts of oil. How much do three boxes weigh in pounds?
